job.title: Codex Task
job.summary: major [state-integrity/redesign]: D2’s prefix-hash map does not provide “distinct chat sessions => distinct conversationIds.” Failure scenario: two native chat sessions in the same org ask the same first prompt and receive the same assistant answer. Both produce the same stored prefix hash; the later write overwrites the earlier conversationId. A follow-up in the first chat then continues the second backend conversation, violating criterion 7 isolation.

request.prompt:
REVIEW-ONLY: validate the Layer-1 implementation SEQUENCE and the bound architecture decisions for a VS Code extension feature. Do NOT modify any code or files. Reply with the single word AGREE on its own line if you have no critical/major objections, otherwise list concrete objections.

Search for issues along ONLY these dimensions:
- correctness: logic bugs, wrong behavior vs spec/acceptance criteria
- race-conditions: concurrency, ordering, shared-state, atomicity, TOCTOU
- simplicity: over-engineering, needless abstraction, code that could be much smaller
- redesign: structural: wrong shape, duplicated source of truth, missing canonical location
- conventions: repo idioms, naming, reusing existing utilities instead of new code
- error-handling: failure paths, edge/boundary inputs, swallowed errors
- security: injection, secrets, untrusted-input handling
- state-integrity: durable-state corruption, partial-write/crash safety, idempotent resume
- api-contract: interface drift vs agreed spec; breaking callers
- test-adequacy: tests that don't exercise the change; missing failure-path tests

Tag every finding with a severity (critical / major / suggestion). Evidence rule: every critical/major finding MUST cite the artifact section plus a concrete failure scenario. A finding without that evidence is a suggestion. Do NOT flag: style preferences; theoretical risks needing unlikely preconditions; defense-in-depth when primary defense is adequate; refactors outside scope; anything off the dimension list. Only critical/major objections block; convergence = zero critical/major, NOT zero findings.

## Goal
Today Rewst Buddy's AI ("RoboRewsty") is a VS Code chat PARTICIPANT (`@rewst`). The chat view only works when a language model is available, which in practice needs a GitHub Copilot sign-in/plan. We are RE-IMPLEMENTING RoboRewsty as a native chat MODEL via the VS Code `LanguageModelChatProvider` API (finalized 1.104; the Copilot sign-in/plan requirement for extension-contributed models was removed in 1.122). Outcome: RoboRewsty appears in the chat model picker and works signed-out, with full parity to the participant. The `@rewst` participant is retired.

## Existing code (reused, not rebuilt)
- `src/sessions/conversation/ConversationClient.ts` exports `askRewstAi(options)` — an async generator over the `conversationMessage` graphql-ws subscription yielding typed events (`registered|conversation|status|chunk|complete|approval|error`); `complete` carries `content` + `sources`; `approval` carries `tools` + `requestId`; backend is stateful via `conversationId`. This streaming path is preserved verbatim.
- `src/ui/chat/RewstChatParticipant.ts` (604 lines) — the participant being retired. Contains the loop logic we port: tool-round loop, approval re-ask via addAllowedTool/removeAllowedTool, org/session resolution, apply buttons, source rendering.
- `src/ui/chat/tools/toolProtocol.ts` — a TEXT tool protocol: appends instructions to the message describing local tools; RoboRewsty replies with fenced ```rewst-tool JSON blocks; `parseToolRequests` extracts them; results fed back as the next turn. `RequestDeduper` guards cycles. This text protocol is how RoboRewsty (which knows nothing about VS Code) calls tools — it must stay.
- Tool spec/run modules: `tools/workspaceTools.ts` (WORKSPACE_TOOL_SPECS, EDIT_TOOL_SPECS, runToolRequests), `tools/webTools.ts`, `tools/commandTool.ts`, `tools/graphqlTool.ts`. Local execution logic reused.
- `src/commands/ui/ApplyRewstAiEdit.ts` — applies a code block to a file via a diff preview shown BEFORE write; has its own unit test asserting preview-before-write.
- Settings (unchanged names/semantics): `rewst-buddy.ai.enableWorkspaceTools | enableEditTools | enableWebTools | enableCommandTool | enableGraphqlTool | maxToolRounds | conversationType | customInstructions | autoApproveCommands`.
- Repo conventions: singleton managers (`X = new (class X {})()`), `@sessions/@ui/@models/@utils/@global` path aliases, colocated `*.test.ts` (mocha), `createMockSession`/`Fixtures` test helpers, `SessionManager.getActiveSessions()/getSessionForOrg(orgId)`.

## Bound architecture decisions (please attack these)
- D1 Model identity / multi-session: register ONE `LanguageModelChatInformation` per active session-org (id derived from orgId). Single session => one model (auto-selected). Multiple sessions => one model per org in the picker. Distinct org model => distinct backend conversation mapping (gives criterion-7 isolation for free). No separate org picker inside a turn.
- D2 Stateless->stateful conversation mapping: the provider API is stateless (it passes the full message array each call); RoboRewsty is stateful (`conversationId`). Strategy: a per-provider in-memory map keyed by a stable hash of the conversation PREFIX (all messages EXCEPT the newest user turn). First turn: no prefix-key match => start a new backend conversation, then store hash(history-including-this-answer) -> conversationId. Next turn: its prefix (history minus newest) hashes to the previously stored key => reuse that conversationId. Distinct chat sessions/orgs produce distinct prefixes/keys => distinct conversationIds. Resume (D6) pre-seeds an entry. Criterion 7 binds the behavior with unit tests.
- D3 Tool ownership: expose all FIVE local capabilities (workspace-read, edit, web, command, graphql) as our OWN registered VS Code language-model tools (`package.json` `languageModelTools` + `vscode.lm.registerTool`), each gated by its existing `rewst-buddy.ai.*` setting. NO delegation to built-in agent tools (keeps settings semantics exact). The tool->setting mapping is recorded once in DECISIONS.md.
- D4 Tool-call translation: provider declares `capabilities.toolCalling`. For each request, the tools VS Code passes in `options.tools` are filtered by the existing settings semantics, then injected into RoboRewsty via the EXISTING text protocol (`buildToolInstructions`). RoboRewsty's `rewst-tool` fenced replies are parsed (`parseToolRequests`) and emitted as `LanguageModelToolCallPart`s; the harness runs the tool and returns `LanguageModelToolResultPart`s in the next message array, which the provider folds back into the next backend turn (`formatToolResults`). A tool whose governing setting is disabled is NEVER injected even if present in `options.tools`. A parsed request whose name is not in `options.tools` is returned as text/error content, never a stalled tool call.
- D5 Approvals: on an `approval` event, the provider surfaces a MODAL `showInformationMessage` with Approve once / Always allow / Cancel. Approve => `addAllowedTool` then continue the SAME provider response inline (provider owns its loop, so no participant-style button/re-open hack needed); Approve-once reverts via `removeAllowedTool` after the turn; Always-allow persists. Criterion 6 binds behavior.
- D6 Conversation resume: a new command `Resume Rewst AI Conversation` (model providers have no slash commands) lists/loads a prior conversation, renders its transcript, and pre-seeds the D2 map so the NEXT provider turn for that org continues that `conversationId` rather than starting fresh. Criteria 7b/8 bind behavior.
- D7 Apply-to-file: retain `ApplyRewstAiEdit` as the diff-preview-before-write apply path (its unit test guards the invariant for criterion 9); native chat UI code-block affordances provide the user-facing trigger.
- D8 Source citations: port the participant's source rendering (URL sources as references, non-URL as a labeled list) into the provider response (criterion 10).

## Proposed task SEQUENCE (each = one independently-committable, independently-verifiable unit; one per review objection later)
1. 001 Raise `engines.vscode` to `^1.122.0` and add the `languageModelChatProviders` contribution (vendor+displayName) to package.json. Done-check: grep/unit asserts engines floor (criterion 3 partial). depends_on: none
2. 002 Add `languageModelTools` declarations to package.json for the five local tools (names mirror tool specs). depends_on: 001
3. 003 Register the five tools via `vscode.lm.registerTool`, each gated by its `rewst-buddy.ai.*` setting; reuse existing run modules. Unit: enabled setting => tool registered, disabled => withheld (criterion 4). depends_on: 002
4. 004 Conversation-mapping module (D2): pure module + unit tests for same-session reuse, post-resume reuse seed, two-session isolation (criterion 7 a/c). depends_on: 001
5. 005 Tool-call translation module (D4): inject settings-filtered `options.tools` via text protocol; parse rewst-tool replies -> ToolCallParts; fold ToolResultParts -> next turn; settings filter; undeclared-tool fallback. Unit tests for each (criterion 5). depends_on: 003
6. 006 The `LanguageModelChatProvider` (D1): `provideLanguageModelChatInformation` (per-org models, toolCalling capability) + `provideLanguageModelChatResponse` wiring `askRewstAi` streaming -> text parts, using 004+005; register via `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` in extension.ts + package.json. Unit: model-info per session; streaming via mocked askRewstAi (criteria 1,2,7b). depends_on: 004,005
7. 007 Approvals in provider mode (D5): approval event -> modal -> addAllowedTool/revert -> inline resume. Unit: mapping + revert (criterion 6). depends_on: 006
8. 008 `Resume Rewst AI Conversation` command (D6): list/load/transcript + pre-seed map. Unit of listing/loading + continuity (criteria 7b,8). depends_on: 006
9. 009 Source citations in provider response (D8). Unit of formatting (criterion 10). depends_on: 006
10. 010 Apply-to-file (D7): keep ApplyRewstAiEdit preview-before-write; ensure provider answers' code blocks are appliable; unit asserts preview before write (criterion 9). depends_on: 006
11. 011 Retire `@rewst`: remove `chatParticipants` contribution + RewstChatParticipant init/registration + dead code. Grep test (criterion 11). depends_on: 007,008,009,010
12. 012 Docs + CHANGELOG (docs/features.md, docs/reference.md, README glance, CHANGELOG) + full `npm run test:unit` green (criteria 12,13). depends_on: 011

## Questions for you
1. Is the SEQUENCE correct and minimal — any task that must come earlier/later, any missing dependency, any criterion (1-13) not covered by a task?
2. Are D1-D8 sound? Especially: D2 prefix-hash mapping correctness/race under interleaved sessions; D4 settings-filter + undeclared-tool fallback completeness; D5 modal-in-provider acceptability; D6 resume seeding the map without a chat-session id.
3. Any task too large for a single focused implementation pass (should be split)?

result.rawOutput:
major [state-integrity/redesign]: D2’s prefix-hash map does not provide “distinct chat sessions => distinct conversationIds.” Failure scenario: two native chat sessions in the same org ask the same first prompt and receive the same assistant answer. Both produce the same stored prefix hash; the later write overwrites the earlier conversationId. A follow-up in the first chat then continues the second backend conversation, violating criterion 7 isolation.

major [correctness/api-contract]: D6 resume cannot be made reliable with only “pre-seed the D2 map” and no chat-session id or injected durable marker. Failure scenario: the command loads conversation A, but the next provider call has an empty or unrelated prefix unless the transcript is actually present in the native chat message array. Seeding an empty/org-level prefix risks the next unrelated first turn continuing conversation A; seeding the transcript prefix will not match unless VS Code has that transcript as real chat history.

major [api-contract/correctness]: D3/sequence 002 says “five local tools,” but the existing text protocol has many concrete tool names (`read_file`, `list_files`, `edit_file`, `web_search`, `rewst_graphql`, etc.). Failure scenario: if package.json/registerTool declares only five category tools, D4 emits `LanguageModelToolCallPart("read_file", ...)`, but VS Code has no registered `read_file` tool to execute, so tool parity breaks. The registered tool names need to match every protocol tool name or D4 needs an explicit wrapper translation design.

major [test-adequacy/api-contract]: sequence 001 raises `engines.vscode` but does not include updating `@types/vscode`/lockfile to the API floor. Failure scenario: step 006 implements `LanguageModelChatProvider` against the 1.122 API while the repo still compiles against `@types/vscode` 1.107, so `npm run compile:test` fails despite the engine grep passing.

major [correctness/test-adequacy]: D7/010 does not actually bind native chat code-block affordances to `ApplyRewstAiEdit`. Failure scenario: provider output renders a code block, user clicks VS Code’s native apply/insert affordance, and that path does not call the extension’s diff-preview-before-write command. The existing `ApplyRewstAiEdit` unit test still passes while criterion 9 is not exercised for provider answers.

Source checked for API surface: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/vscode/main/src/vscode-dts/vscode.d.ts
